summary: Critical issues prevent this from shipping: (1) Workflow PowerShell installation (lines 19-28) uses deprecated apt-key and will fail on ubuntu-latest. (2) Uses deprecated ::set-output syntax; should use $GITHUB_OUTPUT. (3) Integration test script run-integration-tests.ps1 is never invoked by workflow—tests don't actually run via act as required. (4) Missing permissions: block. (5) semantic-version-bumper.ps1 lacks input validation, doesn't handle pre-release versions or non-3-part semvers. (6) bump-version.ps1 commit resolution logic (lines 9-25) is fragile and under-documented. (7) Naive regex output parsing in run-integration-tests.ps1 (lines 65-75) will break on output format changes. (8) Hard-coded commits.txt fallback (line 52) isn't guaranteed to exist. Positives: Pester tests well-structured, function organization reasonable, cross-platform path handling present. Needs: Fix PowerShell install, modernize GitHub Actions syntax, actually invoke integration tests, add semantic version validation.
